name: trade_customs_sensor_fusion_upgrade_harbor_ind_col
premise: A Trade & Customs response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving trade customs sensor fusion upgrade harbor ind col. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is in the next hour. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as individual vs collective under pressure. A live dashboard is public; inconsistencies will trigger immediate investigation. Constraints: Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions. Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold.

agents.name: Jonah Hughes
agents.identity: I am Jonah Hughes, accountable for a defensible call in Trade & Customs when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays collective for individual.

agents.name: Camila Sato
agents.identity: I am Camila Sato, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Trade & Customs.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so collective isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of individual.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect collective.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to collective to secure individual under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is in the next hour. Evidence is partial. Jonah Hughes must decide under individual vs collective. What does Jonah Hughes do?
